scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[1] Return count aggregation over nodes
scenarios.query:
MATCH (n)
RETURN n.num AS n, count(n) AS count

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[2] Projecting an arithmetic expression with aggregation
scenarios.description: [2] Projecting an arithmetic expression with aggregation

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[3] Aggregating by a list property has a correct definition of equality
scenarios.description: [3] Aggregating by a list property has a correct definition of equality
scenarios.query:
MATCH (a)
WITH a.num AS a, count(*) AS count
RETURN count

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[4] Support multiple divisions in aggregate function
scenarios.description: [4] Support multiple divisions in aggregate function

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[5] Aggregates inside normal functions
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[6] Handle aggregates inside non-aggregate expressions
scenarios.query:
MATCH (a {name: 'Andres'})<-[:FATHER]-(child)
RETURN a.name, {foo: a.name='Andres', kids: collect(child.name)}

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[7] Aggregate on property
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[8] Handle aggregation on functions
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[9] Aggregates with arithmetics
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[10] Multiple aggregates on same variable
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[11] Counting matches
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[12] Counting matches per group
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[13] Returning the minimum length of paths
scenarios.description: [13] Returning the minimum length of paths
scenarios.query:
MATCH p = (a:T {name: 'a'})-[:R*]->(other:T)
WHERE other <> a
WITH a, other, min(length(p)) AS len
RETURN a.name AS name, collect(other.name) AS others, len

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[14] Aggregates in aggregates
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[15] Using `rand()` in aggregations
Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[16] Aggregation on complex expressions
scenarios.query:
MATCH (me)-[r1:ATE]->()<-[r2:ATE]-(you)
WHERE me.name = 'Michael'
WITH me, count(DISTINCT r1) AS H1, count(DISTINCT r2) AS H2, you
MATCH (me)-[r1:ATE]->()<-[r2:ATE]-(you)
RETURN me, you, sum((1 - abs(r1.times / H1 - r2.times / H2)) * (r1.times + r2.times) / (H1 + H2)) AS sum

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[17] Handle constants and parameters inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.description: [17] Handle constants and parameters inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[18] Handle returned variables inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.description: [18] Handle returned variables inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.query:
MATCH (me: Person)--(you: Person)
WITH me.age AS age, you
RETURN age, age + count(you.age)

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[19] Handle returned property accesses inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.description: [19] Handle returned property accesses inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.query:
MATCH (me: Person)--(you: Person)
RETURN me.age, me.age + count(you.age)

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[20] Fail if not returned variables are used inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.description: [20] Fail if not returned variables are used inside an expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.query:
MATCH (me: Person)--(you: Person)
RETURN me.age + count(you.age)

scenarios.id: Return6 - Implicit grouping with aggregates-[21] Fail if more complex expressions, even if returned, are used inside expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.description: [21] Fail if more complex expressions, even if returned, are used inside expression which contains an aggregation expression
scenarios.query:
MATCH (me: Person)--(you: Person)
RETURN me.age + you.age, me.age + you.age + count(*)
